title: eremophila abietina subsp. abietina
intro: Eremophila abietina subsp. abietina, also known as the 'Bitterbush', is a shrub native to Australia that belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits in reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and soothing respiratory issues. Eremophila abietina subsp. abietina can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions. The plant's essential oils and extracts have also been studied for their potential therapeutic applications, including as antimicrobial agents and anti-inflammatory compounds.
